stories.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
stories.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned

stories.title: ICE Poses a Real Threat to Our Elections
stories.description:
DHS has built a national police force with massive surveillance capabilities — which it could use to interfere with our elections. 
 The post  ICE Poses a Real Threat to Our Elections  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Weasel Words: OpenAI’s Pentagon Deal Won’t Stop AI‑Powered Surveillance
stories.description: OpenAI, the maker of ChaptGPT, is rightfully facing widespread criticism for its decisions to fill the gap the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) created when rival Anthropic    refused to    drop its restrictions against using its AI for surveillance and autonomous weapons systems. stories.title: Workers report watching Ray-Ban Meta-shot footage of people using the bathroom
stories.description: Meta accused of "concealing the facts" about smart glass users' privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
stories.description: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

stories.title: FBI investigating hack on its wiretap and surveillance systems: Report
stories.description: Hackers allegedly broke into the FBI’s networks, according to a report by CNN.

stories.title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.4 with more knowledge-work capability
stories.description: Updates come amid user blowback over the company's Pentagon deal.

stories.title: The Controversies Finally Caught Up to Kristi Noem
stories.description: Donald Trump said he would replace the secretary of the Department of Homeland Security. Noem’s tenure was marked by aggressive anti-immigration tactics and ICE and CBP’s killing of two US protesters.

stories.title: Military Leaders See Iran War as “God’s Divine Plan” — a Chilling Turn for Trump’s Fascism
stories.description:
A military watchdog has been “inundated” with complaints that officials are using end-times Christian rhetoric to justify war. 
 The post  Military Leaders See Iran War as “God’s Divine Plan” — a Chilling Turn for Trump’s Fascism  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online
stories.description:
The bipartisan push to remove anonymity from the internet is ushering in an era of unprecedented mass surveillance and censorship. 
 The post  Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
stories.description: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

stories.title: U.S. Military Joins Drug War in Ecuador: “It Wasn’t Going to Be Just Boat Strikes Forever”
stories.description:
Two government officials told The Intercept that the joint U.S.–Ecuador military action won’t just be a one-off raid.

stories.title: Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety
stories.description:
Read our letter to the Board of Police Commissioners exposing LAPD's partnership with Flock Safety and its harms to our communities. Join our fight to shut down Flock!  
 The post  Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety  appeared first on  Stop LAPD Spying Coalition .

stories.title: The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes
stories.description:
The Iran war shows that Trump is loving his military interventions — but they are never what he claims them to be. 
 The post  The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: “Free” Surveillance Tech Still Comes at a High and Dangerous Cost
stories.description: Surveillance    technology    vendors, federal agencies, and wealthy private donors have long helped provide local law enforcement “free” access to surveillance equipment that bypasses local oversight.
